nameMeanings.name: Vernon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Vernon in Eure, France, named from the Gaulish element ver(n) ‘alder’ + the Gallo-Roman locative suffix -o (genitive -ōnis).
French: habitational name from the same place as in 1 above or from any of several other places in France with the same name and etymology.

variants.name: Varnon
meanings: English (Leicestershire and Northamptonshire): variant of   Vernon  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Vernon was born in 1312, in Haddon Hall, Derbyshire, England, his father, Richard de Vernon II, was 27 and his mother, Matilda De Camville Baroness Vernon, was 29. He died in 1352, in his hometown, at the age of 40.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William Vernon est né en 1312, à Haddon Hall, Derbyshire, Angleterre, son père, Richard de Vernon II, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Matilda De Camville Baroness Vernon, avait 29 ans. Il est décédé en 1352, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 40 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William Vernon 1312 in Haddon Hall, Derbyshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard de Vernon II 27 und seine Mutter Matilda De Camville Baroness Vernon 29. Er starb 1352 im Alter von 40 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William Vernon è nato il nel 1312 nel Haddon Hall, Derbyshire, England, il suo padre, Richard de Vernon II, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Matilda De Camville Baroness Vernon, aveva 29 anni. Morì nel 1352, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 40 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Vernon(이)가 1312년 영국 더비실르 하던 홀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard de Vernon II(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Matilda De Camville Baroness Vernon(은)는 29세였다. 그는 1352년 그의 고향에서 40세의 나이로 사망했다.
